mission.title: Empowering Communities Through Spiritual Responsibility
mission.paragraphOne: At Alokayon, we believe charity is more than just a transaction; it is a profound act of stewardship. We bridge the gap between those with the means to give and those with the capacity to thrive.
mission.paragraphTwo: Our model prioritizes sustainable change, from direct community support to long-term educational opportunity, all grounded in the principles of equity, dignity, and transparency.
mission.quote: The best of people are those who are most useful to others.

donation.title: Your donation can become relief, education, and hope.
donation.description: Stand with Alokayon in supporting students, widows, elderly people, and families in hardship through trusted and transparent charitable work.

contact.title: Join with us to help others
contact.description: Leave your contact information and we’ll reach out about ways to support Alokayon through giving, volunteering, or community work.

hero.title: Serving people with care, trust, and dignity.
hero.description: Alokayon is a non-profit, non-political charity in Bangladesh working to support underprivileged communities through education, healthcare, and compassionate relief.

journey.title: A quiet beginning, a formal foundation, and a growing commitment to service.
journey.description: From a small initiative to a trusted charitable organization.
journey.registrationDescription: Formal registration completed in 2019 under the Department of Social Services.

purpose.title: Compassion shaped into steady action.
purpose.paragraphOne: Alokayon was founded on the belief that no one should be left behind in hardship.
purpose.paragraphTwoPrefix: Since 2011, we have supported orphans, widows, and vulnerable families through compassion and collective effort, helping them build independent,

items.title: To serve vulnerable people through compassionate, direct support.
items.text: Alokayon exists to stand beside hard-core poor communities through zakat- and donation-funded support in education, healthcare, widow and elderly care, disability assistance, and dignified relief.
bullets: Education support for underprivileged and meritorious students
Support for widows, elderly people, and persons with disabilities

items.title: A more dignified future for families living through hardship.
items.text: The long-term vision is a society where poverty does not force children out of school, widows are not left without support, and vulnerable families can move toward stability with dignity and hope.
bullets: No one left behind in hardship
Education, care, and shelter within reach
A stronger culture of collective responsibility

items.title: To turn limited resources into meaningful, practical impact.
items.text: With support from compassionate donors, Alokayon focuses on direct educational support, slum-school assistance in Dhaka, help for needy elderly people and widows, support for Hifz students, and relief during disasters such as floods, winter hardship, refugee crises, and public-health emergencies.
bullets: Support where the need is immediate and real
Respond within limited means but with consistency
Keep charity rooted in accountability and care

executive.title: A trusted leadership team guiding Alokayon's service and direction.
executive.description: Alokayon is supported by a 14-member executive committee that helps steer the organization with accountability, continuity, and care for the communities it serves.
executive.footer: Browse through the committee members guiding Alokayon's direction and long-term service.

featured.title: Alokayon Pathshala
featured.description: Alokayon Pathshala is a free school established in Rasulbag Slum, Nakhalpara, Dhaka, to bring educational opportunity to underprivileged children living where hardship is most visible. It reflects Alokayon’s belief that education should reach children where the need is immediate, not only where systems are already strong.
points: Free learning support for children in slum communities
A bridge into regular education, dignity, and long-term opportunity
Part of a wider commitment to school, college, university, and Hifz support

caseStudy.title: Real Impact: A family’s journey from crisis to stability.
caseStudy.problem: Many vulnerable families survive on modest daily earnings. When the primary breadwinner dies, that fragile stability often disappears at once, leaving widowed mothers and children without direction or protection.
caseStudy.reality: In these moments, many fall into debt traps through installment-based loans that deepen poverty instead of solving it. Families can lose assets, housing, and even dignity while trying to survive.
caseStudy.intervention: Alokayon identifies such families and responds through educational support, direct financial help, livelihood tools, and continued attention to the root causes of hardship.
caseStudy.impact: With timely support, families begin to regain stability, children continue their education, and dependence on exploitative debt is reduced.

hero.title: A free educational institution for underprivileged children.
hero.description: Alokayon School was created to bring education, structure, and hope to children growing up in deprivation. What began with one teacher teaching slum children in a veranda became a formal learning space supported by Alokayon.

items.label: Children admitted to government primary schools

whyMatters.title: Education as an interruption to neglect.
whyMatters.paragraphOne: Children carry the potential of tomorrow’s poets, artists, scientists, teachers, and leaders. Yet many underprivileged children grow up without access to food, shelter, healthcare, or education.
whyMatters.paragraphTwo: Alokayon School exists to interrupt that cycle early so children are not pushed toward neglect, illiteracy, or harmful environments, but instead grow with dignity, discipline, and possibility.

founding.title: From a veranda lesson to a formal learning space.
founding.paragraphOne: For a long time, Alokayon wanted to create educational opportunities for underprivileged children but lacked the facilities and resources to begin. That changed when Jesmin Akter, who had been teaching slum children on the veranda of her house, came into contact with the organization.
founding.paragraphTwo: Alokayon responded by renting a small tin-shed room, arranging seating, and providing books, notebooks, and pens. The school was then formally inaugurated as Alokayon Pathshala on 13 February 2023.

timeline.label: Veranda teaching
timeline.title: Jesmin Akter began teaching children informally at her home.

timeline.label: Shared support
timeline.title: Alokayon rented a small tin-shed room and arranged supplies and seating.

timeline.label: Formal launch
timeline.title: Alokayon Pathshala was officially inaugurated on 13 February 2023.

teacher.eyebrow: Meet the teacher behind the school
teacher.title: One person’s persistence became a place of learning.
teacher.paragraphOne: Jesmin Akter’s story gives the page its heart. After losing her father as a child, facing financial hardship, and being pushed into a difficult early marriage, she still held onto her dream of teaching. She began by inviting neighborhood children to study, even using unused pages from her sons’ old notebooks.
teacher.paragraphTwo: Later, with Alokayon’s support, that small effort became a proper school for children who otherwise might never have stepped into a classroom.
teacher.quote: With Alokayon’s support, I now believe I can bring underprivileged children into the light of education, Insha’Allah.
teacher.name: Jesmin Akter

It also notes that educational support is a wider Alokayon priority, with around 200 children benefiting from support programs overall.

hero.title: Abdul Karim Miazi Hafezi Madrasa
hero.description: A distinctive educational institution integrating Arabic and Bengali education to shape students in faith, discipline, and practical academic readiness.

vision.eyebrow: A New Model of Madrasa Education
vision.title: Rooted in deen, prepared for the modern world.
vision.paragraphOne: This madrasa was established to move beyond the conventional system and introduce a modern, time-appropriate curriculum that integrates Arabic and Bengali education.
vision.paragraphTwo: The goal is not only to produce students with strong religious foundations, but also to equip them with essential academic knowledge needed for the modern world.

leadership.eyebrow: Leadership Built on Sacrifice and Integrity
leadership.title: A rare model of accountability in educational service.
leadership.description: The institution is led under the supervision of Mr. Abdul Matin (B.Sc.), Vice President of Alokayon. What makes this madrasa truly distinctive is not only its structure, but the integrity behind how it is sustained.
leadership.imageAlt: Leadership and supervision at the madrasa
points: Director takes no salary or honorarium
A portion of personal pension is donated to support operations
Operational spending is personally supervised with transparency

features.title: Structured learning with spiritual and academic balance.

items.title: Integrated Curriculum
items.text: Arabic, Bengali, English, and Mathematics are taught together with balanced attention.

items.title: No Coaching Dependency
items.text: Lessons are completed in class so students are not pushed toward outside tutoring.

items.title: Structured Learning System
items.text: Daily monitoring, teacher supervision, and regular counseling help keep learning on track.

items.title: Moral & Spiritual Development
items.text: Qur’an memorization, discipline, and a respectful environment shape both mind and character.

items.title: Transparent Management
items.text: Operations are run with accountability, careful oversight, and an unusual commitment to integrity.

items.title: Support for the Poor
items.text: Special care is given to orphaned and extremely poor children who need structured educational access.

pathway.summary: Students graduate with both religious and academic foundations, allowing them to continue in mainstream education with confidence.

hero.title: Real stories from the lives transformed through Alokayon.
hero.description: From education to survival, these are not just testimonials. They are journeys shaped by hardship, support, dignity, and human care.

featuredStory.title: From struggle to stability
featuredStory.cardDescription: A long journey from uncertainty and hunger to education, employment, and service.
badges: Now Senior Teacher at Alokayon School
featuredStory.summary: Nasim Uddin grew up in financial hardship, often struggling to afford food, housing, and education. Despite strong academic results, continuing his studies in Dhaka felt uncertain and painfully fragile.
featuredStory.summaryTwo: Through Alokayon’s support covering tuition, books, examination fees, and wider family needs, he was able to complete higher education. Today, he gives back as a teacher serving the same kind of children he once stood among.
fullStory: Md. Nasim Uddin came from a lower middle-class family in Maheshpur Upazila of Jhenaidah. His father could not afford the cost of his education, so he worked in the fields and tutored others while trying to continue his own studies.

After passing HSC with a GPA-5, he moved to Dhaka and enrolled in the Economics Department at Titumir College. There he struggled to pay mess rent, food costs, and academic expenses, often going hungry while trying to support his parents at home.

In 2019, after being introduced through his cousin Imran Hossain, Alokayon stepped in with support for examination fees, tuition, books, and broader academic expenses. The organization also supported his parents medically, repaired their house after cyclone damage, and extended other forms of assistance including Qard Hasanah.

He later completed both Honors and Master’s degrees, passed the preliminary stage of the 46th BCS examination in 2024, and is now serving as a Senior Teacher at Alokayon Pathshala-1 in Nakhalpara, Dhaka.

impactSummary.title: Emotion grounded in evidence.
points: Education support can change the direction of a life, not just a school year.
Small financial assistance often creates long-term stability and self-reliance.
Transparent, compassionate support builds lasting trust with both beneficiaries and donors.
Community-based intervention multiplies impact because it responds to real needs early.
